title: agents.yaml
description: Schema for aqm (aqm) pipeline configuration files. Defines agents, their handoffs, gates, MCP servers, and parameterization.

apiVersion.description: API version identifier. Currently must be "aqm/v0.1".

entry_point.description: How to select the starting agent. 'first': always use the first agent in the list (default). 'auto': LLM analyzes the user input and picks the best starting agent.

params.description: Parameter declarations for pipeline parameterization. Values are referenced via ${{ params.<name> }} syntax. Supports shorthand (just a default value) or full ParamDefinition objects.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: a bare value is treated as the default. E.g. params: { model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" }

imports.description: Import agents from external YAML files. Imported agents are available as extends targets and are prepended to the agent list.

agents.description: List of agent definitions that form the pipeline.

ParamDefinition.description: A full parameter declaration with type, default, required flag, description, interactive prompt, and auto-detection.

type.description: The parameter type. Used for coercion when resolving CLI overrides.

default.description: Default value for the parameter. Type should match the declared type. If omitted and required is false, resolves to null.

required.description: If true, the parameter must be provided via CLI --param or params.yaml override file. Pipeline loading fails if a required param has no value.

description.description: Human-readable description of the parameter. Shown in error messages when a required param is missing.

prompt.description: Interactive question shown to the user when this param has no value during `aqm run`. E.g. "What is the primary brand color?". When set, the CLI presents a choice: enter manually or auto-detect.

auto_detect.description: LLM instruction for auto-detecting the param value from the project. E.g. "Analyze the project's CSS files and extract the primary color hex code". When set, the interactive prompt offers an auto-detect option that runs this instruction via Claude CLI.

ImportSpec.description: An import directive that pulls agents from an external YAML file. The external file can be a full agents.yaml (with an agents: key) or a bare list of agent dicts.

from.description: Path to the external YAML file, relative to the importing file's directory. E.g. "./shared/qa-agents.yaml".

agents.description: List of agent IDs to import. If empty, all agents from the file are imported. E.g. ["qa_reviewer", "docs_writer"].

ConsensusConfig.description: Consensus detection settings for session nodes.

method.description: How consensus is detected. "vote": each agent says the keyword to agree. "moderator_decides": only the moderator/summary_agent can declare consensus.

keyword.description: Keyword agents use to signal agreement. Case-insensitive matching. E.g. "VOTE: AGREE", "I AGREE", "CONSENSUS_REACHED".

require.description: How many agents must agree. "all": every participant. "majority": more than half.

require_chunks_done.description: When true, consensus is only reached when all chunks are marked done AND the vote threshold is met.

ChunksConfig.description: Chunk decomposition settings for session nodes. Breaks tasks into trackable work units that agents can add, complete, or remove via output directives.

initial.description: Seed chunks created when the session starts. E.g. ["Set up project structure", "Implement auth flow"].

AgentDefinition.description: Definition of a single agent or session node. When type is "agent" (default), requires a runtime. When type is "session", orchestrates a conversational discussion among participant agents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the agent. Used in handoff targets and CLI commands. E.g. "code_reviewer".

name.description: Human-readable display name. If omitted, auto-generated from id by replacing underscores/hyphens with spaces and title-casing. E.g. "Code Reviewer".

type.description: Node type. "agent" (default) is a regular LLM agent. "session" is a conversational group where multiple agents discuss in rounds until consensus.

runtime.description: Execution runtime. Required for type "agent", not used for type "session". "claude" uses Anthropic Claude CLI. "gemini" uses Google Gemini CLI. "codex" uses OpenAI Codex CLI.

model.description: Model identifier to use for this agent. E.g. "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "claude-opus-4-20250514". If null, the runtime default is used.

system_prompt.description: System prompt passed to the model. Supports ${{ params.X }} substitution and {{ transcript }} for session participants.

handoffs.description: List of handoff rules defining where the output goes after this agent completes.

gate.description: Optional gate configuration. When set, the agent's output must pass through a gate (LLM or human review) before handoffs are executed.

mcp.description: List of MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers available to this agent. Supports shorthand string format (just server name) or full config objects.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: just the server name. E.g. "github" is equivalent to { server: "github" }.

cli_flags.description: Additional CLI flags passed to the runtime CLI. E.g. for Claude: ["--allowedTools", "Edit,Read,Bash"], for Codex: ["--sandbox", "read-only"].

claude_code_flags.description: Deprecated: use cli_flags instead. Additional CLI flags passed to the runtime CLI.

context_strategy.description: What context to inject into {{ context }}. 'none': no context (max savings). 'last_only': previous stage only (~55% savings). 'own': agent's private notes only (~85% savings). 'shared': smart-windowed shared context.md. 'both': shared + agent's notes (default, backward-compatible).

abstract.description: If true, this agent serves as a template only and is filtered out before execution. Use with extends for pipeline composition.

extends.description: ID of a parent agent to inherit fields from via shallow merge. Child fields override parent fields. The parent can be abstract. E.g. "base_reviewer".

participants.description: Session only: list of agent IDs that participate in the conversation. E.g. ["architect", "reviewer", "security"].

turn_order.description: Session only: how turns are ordered. "round_robin" cycles through participants. "moderator" puts summary_agent first each round.

max_rounds.description: Session only: maximum number of discussion rounds before terminating.

consensus.description: Session only: how consensus is detected among participants.

summary_agent.description: Session only: agent ID that produces the final summary after consensus. Also serves as moderator when turn_order is "moderator".

context_window.description: Number of recent pipeline stages to include in full context. 0 means include all stages.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: string sets the mode directly.

human_input.description: Human-in-the-loop input configuration. Allows agents to request input from humans during pipeline execution. Supports shorthand (true or mode string) or full HumanInputConfig object.

chunks.description: Session only: chunk decomposition config. Agents use CHUNK_ADD/DONE/REMOVE directives. Use with consensus.require_chunks_done for completion gating.

Handoff.description: A handoff rule defining how output flows from one agent to another.

to.description: Target agent ID or comma-separated list for fan-out. E.g. "qa_reviewer" or "qa, docs" for parallel handoff.

task.description: Description of the task to pass to the target agent. E.g. "Review the code changes for security issues".

condition.description: When this handoff should trigger. Preset values: "always" (unconditional), "on_approve" (gate approved), "on_reject" (gate rejected), "on_pass" (gate passed), "auto" (agent decides target via HANDOFF: <id> in output). Custom expressions are also supported.

payload.description: Jinja2 template for the payload passed to the target agent. The variable {{ output }} contains the current agent's output.

GateConfig.description: Gate configuration for review/approval before handoff.

type.description: Gate type. "llm" uses an LLM to auto-review. "human" pauses the pipeline and waits for manual approval via CLI (aqm approve/reject).

prompt.description: Prompt for LLM gate evaluation. Ignored for human gates. E.g. "Does this code follow our security guidelines? Reply APPROVE or REJECT."

model.description: Model to use for LLM gate evaluation. If null, uses the default model. E.g. "claude-sonnet-4-20250514".

max_retries.description: Maximum number of times an agent can be retried after gate rejection before the pipeline fails. Prevents infinite reject loops.

HumanInputConfig.description: Configuration for human-in-the-loop interaction. Allows agents to request input from humans during pipeline execution.

enabled.description: Whether human input is active for this agent.

mode.description: When to request human input. 'before': always ask before the agent runs. 'on_demand': agent requests via HUMAN_INPUT directive. 'both': combine before and on_demand.

prompt.description: Custom prompt shown to the user (for 'before' mode). E.g. "What features do you need?".

MCPServerConfig.description: MCP server configuration. Simplified format requires only the server name; detailed format allows custom command, args, and environment.

server.description: MCP server name or identifier. E.g. "github", "filesystem", "postgres".

command.description: Command to start the MCP server. E.g. "npx", "python". If null, the default for the server type is used.

args.description: Arguments passed to the MCP server command. E.g. ["/path/to/dir", "--read-only"].

env.description: Environment variables set for the MCP server process. E.g. { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "${{ params.github_token }}" }.
